title: Conditions
description: Explore branching logic in C++ using if-else statements and switch-cases to handle dynamic game loops.
learningObjectives: Implement if, else if, and else branching logic
Create switch-case blocks for enum states
Explain compile-time optimizations like switch-case jump tables

sections.title: Decision Making in Game Systems
sections.content:
Branching statements allow your code to execute different logic blocks based on runtime circumstances. In games, this represents logic like check triggers, collisions, and state machines. 

Using `switch` statements over large chains of `if-else` is highly recommended for categorical checks (like Enums) because compilers can optimize them into 'jump tables' (O(1) lookups in memory) instead of executing sequential branching tests.

sections.title: State Machines via Switch-Case
